channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Astros will retire Billy Wagner's No. 13 in honor of his induction into Hall of Fame
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Houston Astros all-time saves leader Billy Wagner will have his jersey number retired, an honor that coincides with his induction into the Hall of Fame, the team announced Tuesday. Wagner's No. 13 will be the 10th number retired by the Astros during a ceremony Aug. 16.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Simona Halep announces her retirement at age 33 after two Grand Slam titles and a return from a ban
item.description: Two-time Grand Slam champion Simona Halep announced her retirement from tennis at age 33 after a first-round loss at a tournament in her home country of Romania on Tuesday, ending an injury-filled comeback from a doping suspension.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brittney Griner could face 1-year window to take advantage of new talent on Atlanta Dream roster
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — New Atlanta Dream star Brittney Griner says her one-year deal was based on more than just positioning herself for a bigger contract when the WNBA's new collective bargaining agreement is expected to take effect in one year.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Art of the deal: The biggest trades in Bay Area sports history
item.description: Bay Area teams have a rich history of big-time trades that have included the likes of Joe Montana, Willie Mays, Rickey Henderson and Joe Thornton.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Claressa Shields makes boxing history as first undisputed champion in three weight classes
item.description: Claressa Shields became the first boxer in history to earn the Undisputed World Championship in three different weight classes by scoring a unanimous decision over Danielle Perkins on Sunday, Feb. 2 at Dort Financial Center in Flint, MI.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Hall, Local 4

item.title: Trump will attend the Super Bowl in New Orleans. He'll be the first sitting president to go
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump is going to the Super Bowl in New Orleans on Sunday, the first sitting president to do so. The U.S. Secret Service, which is responsible for protecting the president, said in a statement Tuesday that it has had staff on the ground for days preparing for his visit.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Travis Kelce fined for unsportsmanlike conduct in AFC title game
item.description: While there was no flag thrown on the play, Kelce got hit with an $11,255 fine for unsportsmanlike conduct after a one-yard Mahomes rushing touchdown
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, NewsNation

item.title: Former NFL player Rashard Cook looks to shape next generation at Mater Dei
item.description: "We had great experiences past high school, and I want to recreate that for the guys that want that experience.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: CMU Baseball Ethan Nunez gears up for sophomore year
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Just a year ago, Ethan Nunez had no college offers. Now, the Colorado Mesa infielder holds one of the highest honors in the Rocky Mountain Athletic Conference (RMAC). “I couldn’t believe it, honestly,” Nunez said.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Utah HC loses again in overtime, 3-2
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was yet another heartbreaking overtime loss for the Utah Hockey Club. Zach Werenski scored 61 seconds into overtime, and the Columbus Blue Jackets defeated the Utah Hockey Club 3-2 at Delta Center on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl bettors in California have a workaround
item.description: Though Californians looking to place bets on the upcoming Super Bowl LIX are limited by state law banning the practice, a loophole appears to let bettors wager legally.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: MMA fighter praises Hitler, calls him a 'good guy' in podcast
item.description: UFC fighter Bryce Mitchell praised Adolf Hitler and denied the Holocaust happened in the first episode of his new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KTLA 5

item.title: NFL kicker Justin Tucker accused of sexual misconduct by 6 masseuses: report
item.description: Justin Tucker, kicker for the Baltimore Ravens, has been accused by six massage therapists at four separate businesses of sexually inappropriate behavior, the Baltimore Banner reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: One of the Oklahoma's best high school wrestling team's season suspended
item.description: A disciplinary decision from the Oklahoma Secondary Schools Activities Association (OSSAA) has ended the season for the girls wrestling team at Edmond North High School.
item.dc:creator: Sydnee Batzlaff/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Girls Basketball: Francis Parker 46, La Jolla Country Day 45
item.description: KUSI (SAN DIEGO) - Marquee matchup out at Francis Parker as they play host to La Jolla Country Day. Francis Parker's Brianna Mendez and Isabella Riddle would combine for 28 points on the night, leading the charge in their 46-45 win over La Jolla Country Day.. a feat some may had not seen coming.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: BYU outlasts Baylor in overtime, 93-89
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - It was a perfect night for one BYU freshman, and a perfect ending for BYU Tuesday night against Baylor.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Lions agree to hire Broncos' John Morton as OC and promote Kelvin Sheppard to DC, AP source says
item.description: The Detroit Lions have agreed to hire Denver Broncos assistant John Morton as offensive coordinator and promote Kelvin Sheppard to defensive coordinator, according to a person familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5
